title: Authors and Citation
text: Stamm (2026). riot: R Inputs/Outputs Tractography. R package version 1.3.0, https://github.com/astamm/riot.
code: @Manual{,   title = {riot: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography},   author = {Aymeric Stamm},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.3.0},   url = {https://github.com/astamm/riot}, }

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: riot (R Input/Output Tractography) package provides R interface importing exporting tractography data R. Currently supported importing formats : native VTK .vtk .vtp files; medInria .fds files; MRtrix .tck/.tsf files; , TrackVis .trk files. package reads tractography data bundle objects (lists streamline matrices) streamline numeric matrix columns X, Y, Z 3D coordinates successive points. Points may also carry scalar attributes (e.g. diffusion metrics) stored additional matrix columns. package also allows write bundles back following exporting formats: native VTK .vtk .vtp files; , medInria .fds files.

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: can install released version riot CRAN : Alternatively can install development version riot GitHub :

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
code: uf_left_vtk <- read_tractogram(system.file(   "extdata",   "UF_left.vtk",   package = "riot" )) uf_left_vtk #> <bundle [2042 streamlines | 9–33 pts/streamline]> uf_left_vtp <- read_tractogram(system.file(   "extdata",   "UF_left.vtp",   package = "riot" )) uf_left_vtp #> <bundle [2042 streamlines | 9–33 pts/streamline]>

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
code: uf_left_fds <- read_tractogram(system.file(   "extdata",   "UF_left.fds",   package = "riot" )) uf_left_fds #> <bundle [2042 streamlines | 9–33 pts/streamline]>

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
code: af_left_tck <- read_tractogram(system.file(   "extdata",   "AF_left.tck",   package = "riot" )) af_left_tck #> <bundle [5000 streamlines | 8–54 pts/streamline]>

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
code: cc_mid_trk <- read_tractogram(system.file(   "extdata",   "CCMid.trk",   package = "riot" )) cc_mid_trk #> <bundle [525 streamlines | 29–270 pts/streamline]>

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: Since version 1.2.0, riot delegates VTK linkage rvtk infrastructure package. VTK system requirement: neither riot rvtk. install time, rvtk searches pre-installed VTK following order: VTK_DIR environment variable (highest priority). Homebrew — macOS (brew install vtk). pkg-config — macOS Linux. Well-known system prefix paths (/usr, /usr/local) — Linux. Rtools42+ pacman package active MSYS2 environment (e.g. mingw-w64-ucrt-x86_64-vtk UCRT64) — Windows. pre-installed VTK found, rvtk automatically downloads pre-compiled static shared VTK build GitHub releases, manual VTK installation ever required.

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: riot bundles TinyXML-2. tinyxml2.cpp modified avoid use stdout printf per Writing R Extensions manual recommendations R input/output mechanism writing console.

text: streamline numeric matrix whose rows ordered 3-D points along single fibre tract. first three columns must named "X", "Y", "Z". additional columns carry per-point scalar vector attributes.

title: Import tractograms into R — read_tractogram
text: go-function import tractograms R. Based VTK DIPY, currently support eight different formats detailed documentation input argument file.

title: Import tractograms into R — read_tractogram
text: file string specifying path file containing tractography data. Currently supported files : standard VTK formats .vtk .vtp, medInria .fds format, MRtrix .tck/.tsf format, TrackVis .trk .trx formats, DIPY .dpy format, .fib format. reference_file string specifying path reference image file. required importing .trx, .fib, .dpy files, formats contain spatial information image space. reference image used correctly position tractogram appropriate space. Default NULL.

title: Import tractograms into R — read_tractogram
text: bundle object file contains multiple streamlines, streamline object contains exactly one. streamline numeric matrix least three named columns "X", "Y", "Z" (one row per point along tract). Additional per-point scalar attributes, present source file, appear extra named columns.

title: Import tractograms into R — read_tractogram
code: uf_left_vtk <- read_tractogram(system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtk",  package = "riot")) #> Number of data points: 38697 #> Number of streamlines: 2042 #> ✔ The tractogram stored in /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/riot/extdata/UF_left.vtk has been successfully imported.

title: Export tractograms from R — write_tractogram
text: function exports tractogram stored bundle object file one supported formats. Supported formats include VTK (.vtk), VTP (.vtp), FDS (.fds), TRK (.trk), TCK (.tck), TRX (.trx), FIB (.fib), DPY (.dpy). formats require reference image (TRX, FIB, DPY), user must provide path reference image file.

title: Export tractograms from R — write_tractogram
text: x bundle object storing tractogram. file string specifying path file containing tractography data. Currently supported files : standard VTK formats .vtk .vtp, medInria .fds format, MRtrix .tck/.tsf format, TrackVis .trk .trx formats, DIPY .dpy format, .fib format. reference_file string specifying path reference image file. required importing .trx, .fib, .dpy files, formats contain spatial information image space. reference image used correctly position tractogram appropriate space. Default NULL.

text: input tractogram (invisibly) function can used pipes.

title: Export tractograms from R — write_tractogram
code: uf_left  <- read_tractogram(system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtp",  package = "riot")) #> Number of data points: 38697 #> Number of streamlines: 2042 #> v The tractogram stored in /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/riot/extdata/UF_left.vtp has been successfully imported. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ out <- fs::file_temp(ext = ".vtp") write_tractogram(uf_left, file = out) } # }

what: New data model: streamline and bundle objects
title: riot 1.3.0
text: streamline — numeric matrix named columns X, Y, Z (plus optional per-point scalar attribute columns). row ordered point along single tract. PointId implicit row order longer stored. bundle — ordered list streamline objects representing collection tracts. StreamlineId implicit list position longer stored. read_tractogram() now returns streamline file contains exactly one tract, bundle otherwise. write_tractogram() accepts streamline bundle objects. New constructors predicates exported: new_streamline(), is_streamline(), new_bundle(), is_bundle(). print() format() methods provided classes. readr package longer dependency.

what: C++ layer: elimination of intermediate CSV files
title: riot 1.3.0
text: Breaking change (internal): C++ reader functions (ReadVTK, ReadVTP, ReadFDS) longer write temporary CSV file. now return cpp11::writable::list directly R, eliminating temporary file /O associated string-reference issues. C++ writer functions (WriteVTK, WriteVTP, WriteFDS) longer read temporary CSV. now accept cpp11::list directly R. helper WriteCSV (vtkPolyData → CSV) ReadCSV (CSV → vtkPolyData) replaced PolyDataToList ListToPolyData respectively, operating entirely memory.

title: riot 1.3.0
text: VTK discovery, compilation flags, pre-built static library distribution now handled entirely rvtk package (pending CRAN submission). rvtk supplies pre-compiled VTK headers libraries manual VTK installation required platforms: honours user-supplied VTK_DIR environment variable first, tries Homebrew (macOS), pkg-config (macOS/Linux), well-known system prefixes; Windows checks VTK_DIR, Rtools45 pacman, common MSYS2 prefixes; suitable system VTK found downloads pre-built static libraries automatically rvtk GitHub releases. rvtk listed Imports dependency; downstream packages longer need locate VTK . Remotes: astamm/rvtk field removed DESCRIPTION rvtk available CRAN.

title: riot 1.2.0
text: Breaking change system requirements: riot longer bundles VTK source files. Instead, links externally installed VTK (>= 9.1.0) compile time. VTK must present host installing package. shared static VTK builds supported; static builds macOS Linux must compiled -fPIC. user-supplied VTK_DIR environment variable. Homebrew (macOS). pkg-config (macOS Linux). Well-known system include paths (/usr, /usr/local) (Linux). Rtools42+ pacman package active MSYS2 environment (e.g. mingw-w64-ucrt-x86_64-vtk UCRT64) (Windows). Windows, VTK loaded dynamically run time via addDLLDirectory() avoid bundle VTK runtime DLLs inside package. Removed bundled VTK source files, reducing installed package size considerably. Updated SystemRequirements DESCRIPTION document external VTK dependency.

title: riot 1.1.1
text: Modified VTK source files avoid compilation warnings arising using LLVM Apple clang GNU gcc compilers. Now ships shrunk version VTK source files avoid unsuccessful downloads VTK website. Fix compilation errors vtkzlib raised clang16.

title: riot 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-01-06 Update VTK v9.2.4; Avoid prototype checks vtkzlib using LLVM Clang compiler.

title: riot 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-10 first major release, : Added support read MRtrix .tck/.tsf file formats (#5). Added support read TrackVis .trk file formats (#5). Use one core compile VTK compliance CRAN policy (thanks Prof. B. Ripley). Added tilde expansion file paths. make first major release consider popular tractography formats now supported riot. chose design support VTK medInria file formats writing.

title: riot 0.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-05 Added NEWS.md file track changes package. Supports reading writing .vtk, .vtp .fds file formats.
